Both division types use Dematic Pick to Light for picking from repack. Only conveyable divisions have pre-sort lanes, merge lanes and down lanes to dock doors for outbound. In conveyable divisions, some of the picking is done without shipping sorters similar to non-conveyable divisions. This document covers the communication between WM OS and Dematic for DC Operations . Key Decisions/ Assumptions McLane utilizes WMOS201 9 for th is implementation. McLane divisions do not track iLPNs in pick locations . McLane divisions do not track lot or item attributes for PTL picked locations . For company/facility setup, there is one company and one facility. Catch weight is not in scope for this implementation Voice/ VoCollect is not in scope for this implementation McLane may decide to capture LM metrics b y turning on corresponding Warehouse Parameters. When Dematic requests replenishment, WMOS sends replenishment confirmation if the replenishment is successful. No message sent to Dematic if replenishment request cannot be fulfilled. A custom SCI alert informs the outbound supervisor (and corresponding users) about replenishment failure. It’s a manual communication to picker to short the quantity. WMOS cubing is assumed for oLPNs to be picked by PTL . PTL honors oLPNs created by WMOS. WMOS does not send any label printing message to Dematic . Shipments are assigned to dock doors before selecting them for waving in WMOS. This is needed in wave download from WMOS so that Dematic can divert the oLPNs to correct down lane. McLane does not track iLPNs in Active and Case Pick location. All PTL picked Case pick locations and active locations are single item locations except cigarette switch-out locations. WMOS wave configuration is setup to cube each conveyable full case as an oLPN. This is McLane business requirement and existing Dematic processing model. Otherwise, change is required in both WMOS and Dematic to process multi-case oLPN. PTL in this document refers to Dematic system. Maximum length of sort wave number from Dematic is 3 digits. oLPNs for conveyable items and oLPNs for non-conveyable items are mutually exclusive. All locations are managed and replenished by WMOS. E-com orders are picked only through WMOS RF and not by PTL . E-com orders are not diverted through conveyor. WMOS does not send shipping label (like ZPL) and content label to Dematic . WMOS always sends custom MHE Shipment Close message to Dematic and does not check if any PTL oLPN is in the shipment. Dematic ignore s the message if there is no oLPN to update. Parcel store orders are not processed by Dematic conveyor. TBD for conveyable divisions. Dependencies /References WM Customer Flow Document WM OS - Dematic Communication Flow Flow Diagrams This section provides a high-level overview of each of the incoming and outgoing messages, and how those messages are processed within WMOS DIF layer. This section also provides an overview of each of the messages from WMOS to Dematic , as well as the messages from Dematic to WMOS. Work Creation – Non-Conveyable Divisions Wave planner executes wave in WMOS. At the end of the wave, WMOS sends the wave download file with oLPNs only where PTL performs picking. After processing the message, PTL prepares for picking. Messages Wave Download – Non - Conveyable Divisions Work Creation – Conveyable Divisions In conveyable divisions, wave planner executes wave in WMOS. At the end of the wave, WMOS sends the wave download file with all the conveyable oLPNs and only non-conveya ble oLPNs loaded in the same dock door as conveyable oLPNs . This includes conveyable oLPNs picked by WMOS, non-conveyable oLPNs picked by WMOS and all oLPNs picked by PTL. After processing the message, Dematic assigns sort wave number to the oLPNs . Then sends the oLPN with sort wave number to WMOS. WMOS process this custom message through GAP19 to update the oLPN and create picking tasks. Items loaded in n on-conveyable dock door are waved separately and the oLPNs created in that wave are not sent to Dematic for sort wave assignment. These non-conveyable oLPNs are picked and staged like the non-con vey able divisions. Messages Wave Download – Conveyable Divisions oLPNs with Sort wave PTL Picking Inventory for PTL oLPNs is picked by Dematic . This process is common for both conveyable and non-conveyable divisions. Dematic sends update per oLPN line when picking is complete or when picking is paused ( Line Item Status ). If picker is adding oLPN content to new oLPN , the new oLPN number is sent in the packing update message. In that case, the original oLPN is updated to “ packed ” status. The remaining unpacked lines on the original oLPN are assigned to the new oLPN. Upon processing the packing update message, WMOS sends the MHE message to Dematic when oLPN is packed ( oLPN Packed ) . This is for conveyable divisions only . If there is shortage, Dematic may request replenishment for the location. WMOS processes the message and reprioritizes existing replenishments. If none exists, WMOS creates replenishment. If Dematic requirement cannot be fulfilled, no message is sent to Dematic . Upon fill pick in requested location, WMOS sends replenishment complete. Once packing complete, the oLPN is inducted which may be processed for audit by Dematic . During Dematic audit , Dematic sends message if expected inventory cannot be located. Processing this custom message in WMOS is accomplished through EX02 MHE oLPN Adjustments . WMOS performs down adjustments for the oLPN line. If the oLPN content needs to be moved to another oLPN, fully or partially, Dematic sends a message to WMOS for each line moved to each destination oLPN. Processing this custom message in WMOS is accomplished through EX03 MHE Move LPN Content . WMOS performs oLPN line move to the destination oLPN. Messages Line I tem Status oLPN Packed oLPN Down Adjustment oLPN Split-Move oLPN Cancelled Replenishment Request Replenishment Complete WMOS RF Picking Picking in WMOS is executed using RF device and label picking. As an alternative to label picking, McLane picks from active using RF device . Once the conveyable oLPN is packed, WMOS sends oLPN Packed message ( oLPN Packed ) . In case of shortage, oLPN Packed message . Messages oLPN Packed Shipping – Non- Conveyable Divisions In non-conveyable divisions, Dematic palletizes the PTL oLPNs . This information is sent to WMOS. Through EX22 MHE Palletize oLPN , WMOS palletizes the oLPN. Then the pallet is moved to dock door area for loading. T he oLPNs are loaded using WMOS RF device . Once all the oLPNs are loaded, outbound office user closes shipment. EX04 Shipment Close MHE Message sends the custom message from WMOS to Dematic . Messages MHE Palletize oLPN Shipment Closed Shipping –Conveyable Divisions In conveyable divisions, when a conveyable oLPN, either WMOS picked or PTL picked, is inducted, Dematic diverts the oLPN to corresponding dock door. PTL oLPNs are first collected in sorting lane and then released by sort wave. Dematic sends Divert scan in sorting lane to WMOS to track the last known location. Similarly, all conveyable oLPNs including the oLPNs picked using WMOS, are merged in merge lane. . Dematic sends divert scan in merge lane to WMOS to track the last known location. Finally, when the oLPN passes down lane, Dematic send another divert message. Using this message, WMOS updates the oLPN to “Loaded on Truck” status. Messages oLPN Divert Shipment Closed Overview McLane non-conveyable divisions and conveyable divisions are setup with PTL. Conveyable divisions are setup with Dematic MHE automation for outbound pre- sort lanes for PTL oLPNs , sort wave level merge lanes and shipping sorter lanes . WM OS sends wave download messages to Dematic for the Orders to be processed in Dematic . For non- conveyable divisions , oLPNs to be picked for PTL are sent ( Wave Download – Non-Conveyable ) . For conveyable divisions , all oLPNs are sent to Dematic for additional work planning in Dematic . PTL oLPNs are flagged so that Dematic can pick those oLPNs ( Wave Download –Conveyable ) . Dematic plans work in conveyable divisions and sends message to WMOS with all oLPNs and associated sort wave number ( oLPNs with Sort Wave ). Using GAP19, WMOS updates oLPNs with sort wave number and creates picking tasks. Inventory for PTL oLPNs is picked in Dematic . Dematic sends update per oLPN line when picking is complete or when picking is paused ( Line Item Status ) . The quantity on this message is an incremental quantity for the quantity packed but not the total quantity packed for the oLPN line. During Dematic QC, Dematic sends message if expected inventory cannot be located ( oLPN Down Adjustment ) . For shorting the oLPN line completely, Dematic sends the message with quantity as zero. Processing this custom message in WMOS is accomplished through EX02 MHE oLPN Adjustment . WMOS performs down adjustments for the oLPN line. If the oLPN content needs to be moved to another oLPN, fully or partially, Dematic sends a message to WMOS for each line moved to each destination oLPN ( oLPN Split-Move ) . Processing this custom message in WMOS is accomplished through EX03 MHE Move oLPN Content . WMOS performs oLPN line move to the destination oLPN. oLPN Packed Once the conveyable oLPN is packed, WMOS sends oLPN Packed message ( oLPN Packed ) . All full case conveyable oLPNs, label picked/RF picked oLPNs from active and PTL packed oLPNs are considered for this message. MHE Palletize oLPN Dematic sends the message to WMOS when the PTL oLPN is located in pallet ( MHE Palletize oLPN ) . Through EX22 MHE Palletize oLPN , WMOS processes this custom message to palletize the PTL oLPNs . oLPN Divert To track the last known location of the oLPN in conveyer , Dematic sends divert message to WM for tracking. The physical divert is configured as the reserve location WMOS. After processing the message, WMOS locates the oLPN in the physical divert. Similarly, the last divert for oLPN acts as loading divert; WM updates oLPN to “ Loaded on Truck ” upon receiving this divert. Replenishment If additional inventory needed to fulfil PTL oLPNs , Dematic request WM OS ( Replenishment Request ). WM processes the request and sends allocation complete message to Dematic ( Replenishment Complete ) when the fill active is executed on the location . If request cannot be fulfilled, no failure message is sent to Dematic . If oLPN is cancelled in WMOS, base message is sent to Dematic ( oLPN Cancelled ). Undo Wave No systemic message communication between WMOS and Dematic for undo wave . Refer to WMOS CFD document for details. Loading & Shipping Through EX04 Shipment Close MHE Message , WMOS sends a message when shipment is closed in WMOS ( Shipment Close d ). The EVENT_MESSAGE table displays the following statuses throughout message processing: Status Description 5 Receive message unprocessed 10 Send message unprocessed 15 Dematic receive message unprocessed 20 Dematic send message unprocessed 21 Dematic send message unprocessed wave 22 Dematic send message unprocessed shipment 40 Message in progress 90 Message sent 91 Message processed 95 Receive message error 96 Send message error The CL_ENDPOINT_QUEUE table displays the following statuses throughout message processing: Status Description 1 Not ready 2 Ready 3 Submitted 4 Resubmitted 5 Succeeded 6 Failed 10 Busy The CL_MESSAGE table does not have an associated status but contains the message contents and other helpful information. WMOS Dematic Message Configuration This section highlights the configuration requirements for incoming and outgoing Dematic messages within WMOS. Incoming and outgoing Message Configuration will be setup during c onfiguration phase Standard Socket Messages and Formats Basic Delimited Format WMOS maintains the same message formatting for messages sent from Dematic and messages sent from WMOS to Dematic . Each field is delimited by the ‘carat’ (‘^’) character. The below table outlines the basic message format for all Dematic messages. Field Type Max Value Comments for Messages STX ASCII ASCII (02) Start of Message. Message Body Alpha Variable Message Body or Payload Message Body details for each message are included throughout section 5 Dematic Message Details ETX ASCII ASCII (03) End of message. ACK Message Each message sent from WMOS to Dematic or Dematic to WMOS acknowledged back to the sender once the message successfully received. This is accomplished by returning message number back to the sender as shown in the following table.  If the sender does not receive an “ACK” they will retry the message.  If the retry also not received, the ACK message fails.  WM requires a fixed “ACK” format. Messages from WMOS to Dematic : DIF (Device Integration Framework) endpoint will be configured in the connect mode. DIF will connect to the Dematic port to create the socket connection and messages will be send over to Dematic on this socket connection. If DIF do not receive “ACK” on sent message within prescribed time (usually configured as 5000ms), then a resend process initiated. Resends will continue until Max Response Retry Limit (usually configured as 5) is reached. Each resend will be send after 5000ms. After Max number of retries, message will be failed in DIF. Messages from Dematic to WMOS: DIF endpoint configured in listen mode. Dematic will connect to the DIF port to create the socket connection and messages sent over to DIF on this socket connection. DIF will send back the ACK on successful receipt of the message. Field Type Max Value Description/Comments Message Type Alpha Variable MessageType_ACK Note: there are underscores here (not seen with Track Changes Message ID Alpha Variable "Message ID” _ACK Note: there are underscores here (not seen with Track Changes Message Body Na Na Na Empty field Sample Message Message ID: 123456789 [Incoming message ID from Dematic to WMOS, and vice versa] ACK Message String:   <STX>MessageType_ACK^123456789_ACK<ETX> End Point Configuration ACK Data: {MSG:^1}_ACK^{MSG:^2}_ACK Assumption: Message type and message id are 1 st and 2 nd fields in the message . Heartbeat Message Heartbeat is a special message that DIF requires to keep a connection open. DIF sends/receives heartbeat message every 10 seconds (this is a configurable value).  If DIF endpoint configured in connect mode, we can configure DIF to send Heartbeat messages to Dematic at regular intervals. If DIF endpoint configured in listen mode, then Dematic can send Heartbeat messages to DIF at regular intervals. If DIF does not see a Heartbeat, the connection dropped and re-connected.  Configure WM for sending an acknowledgement for a Heartbeat message. Heartbeat is a static message. Sample Message Heartbeat Message String:       <STX>HEARTBEAT<ETX> Heartbeat ACK Message String: <STX>HEARTBEAT_ACK<ETX> End Point Configuration Error Handling and Messaging The sections below illustrate high level error handling scenarios between WMOS and Dematic . Each section gives a brief description of the scenario, how the messages are processed, and any additional ACK/NAK message processing that is required. Error Handling Scenarios – Dematic to WMOS Messaging Messages sent to WMOS from Dematic over socket endpoints can result in two types of error scenarios: Dematic resend the message if the ACK is not received after configured timeout . WM OS may accept the message but may find an error in the business logic processing. In this case, WM logs an appropriate error message. There is no communication from WM back to Dematic with the error message and therefore there is no further Dematic response for this particular scenario. Eventually, the iLPN, oLPN, or tote will get diverted to jackpot or another problem area. Error Handling Scenarios – WMOS to Dematic Messaging Messages sent from WMOS to Dematic over socket endpoints can result in two types of error scenarios DIF resend the message if the ACK is not received after configured timeout . Dematic may accept the message but may find an error in the business logic processing. There is no communication from Dematic back to WM and therefore there is no further WM response. 2.8 7/8/2021 Yamini Pulusu Sequence Number is added to the wave download message Introduction & Purpose McLane is implementing WMOS 2019 globally across all for its divisions, both conveyable and non-conveyable . Both division types use Dematic Pick to Light for picking from repack. Only conveyable divisions have pre-sort lanes, merge lanes and down lanes to dock doors for outbound. In conveyable divisions, some of the picking is done without shipping sorters similar to non-conveyable divisions. This document covers the communication between WM OS and Dematic for DC Operations . Key Decisions/ Assumptions McLane utilizes WMOS201 9 for th is implementation. McLane divisions do not track iLPNs in pick locations . McLane divisions do not track lot or item attributes for PTL picked locations . For company/facility setup, there is one company and one facility. Catch weight is not in scope for this implementation Voice/ VoCollect is not in scope for this implementation McLane may decide to capture LM metrics b y turning on corresponding Warehouse Parameters. When Dematic requests replenishment, WMOS sends replenishment confirmation if the replenishment is successful. No message sent to Dematic if replenishment request cannot be fulfilled. A custom SCI alert informs the outbound supervisor (and corresponding users) about replenishment failure. It’s a manual communication to picker to short the quantity. WMOS cubing is assumed for oLPNs to be picked by PTL . PTL honors oLPNs created by WMOS. WMOS does not send any label printing message to Dematic . Shipments are assigned to dock doors before selecting them for waving in WMOS. This is needed in wave download from WMOS so that Dematic can divert the oLPNs to correct down lane. McLane does not track iLPNs in Active and Case Pick location. All PTL picked Case pick locations and active locations are single item locations except cigarette switch-out locations. WMOS wave configuration is setup to cube each conveyable full case as an oLPN. This is McLane business requirement and existing Dematic processing model. Otherwise, change is required in both WMOS and Dematic to process multi-case oLPN. PTL in this document refers to Dematic system. Maximum length of sort wave number from Dematic is 3 digits. oLPNs for conveyable items and oLPNs for non-conveyable items are mutually exclusive. All locations are managed and replenished by WMOS. E-com orders are picked only through WMOS RF and not by PTL .

[Document: MCLN MHE Communication Document v2.8]

his document refers to Dematic system. Maximum length of sort wave number from Dematic is 3 digits. oLPNs for conveyable items and oLPNs for non-conveyable items are mutually exclusive. All locations are managed and replenished by WMOS. E-com orders are picked only through WMOS RF and not by PTL .

E-com orders are not diverted through conveyor. WMOS does not send shipping label (like ZPL) and content label to Dematic . WMOS always sends custom MHE Shipment Close message to Dematic and does not check if any PTL oLPN is in the shipment. Dematic ignore s the message if there is no oLPN to update. Parcel store orders are not processed by Dematic conveyor. TBD for conveyable divisions. Dependencies /References WM Customer Flow Document WM OS - Dematic Communication Flow Flow Diagrams This section provides a high-level overview of each of the incoming and outgoing messages, and how those messages are processed within WMOS DIF layer. This section also provides an overview of each of the messages from WMOS to Dematic , as well as the messages from Dematic to WMOS. Work Creation – Non-Conveyable Divisions Wave planner executes wave in WMOS. At the end of the wave, WMOS sends the wave download file with oLPNs only where PTL performs picking. After processing the message, PTL prepares for picking. Messages Wave Download – Non - Conveyable Divisions Work Creation – Conveyable Divisions In conveyable divisions, wave planner executes wave in WMOS. At the end of the wave, WMOS sends the wave download file with all the conveyable oLPNs and only non-conveya ble oLPNs loaded in the same dock door as conveyable oLPNs . This includes conveyable oLPNs picked by WMOS, non-conveyable oLPNs picked by WMOS and all oLPNs picked by PTL. After processing the message, Dematic assigns sort wave number to the oLPNs . Then sends the oLPN with sort wave number to WMOS. WMOS process this custom message through GAP19 to update the oLPN and create picking tasks. Items loaded in n on-conveyable dock door are waved separately and the oLPNs created in that wave are not sent to Dematic for sort wave assignment. These non-conveyable oLPNs are picked and staged like the non-con vey able divisions. Messages Wave Download – Conveyable Divisions oLPNs with Sort wave PTL Picking Inventory for PTL oLPNs is picked by Dematic . This process is common for both conveyable and non-conveyable divisions. Dematic sends update per oLPN line when picking is complete or when picking is paused ( Line Item Status ). If picker is adding oLPN content to new oLPN , the new oLPN number is sent in the packing update message. In that case, the original oLPN is updated to “ packed ” status. The remaining unpacked lines on the original oLPN are assigned to the new oLPN.

[Document: MCLN MHE Communication Document v2.8]

or when picking is paused ( Line Item Status ). If picker is adding oLPN content to new oLPN , the new oLPN number is sent in the packing update message. In that case, the original oLPN is updated to “ packed ” status. The remaining unpacked lines on the original oLPN are assigned to the new oLPN.

Upon processing the packing update message, WMOS sends the MHE message to Dematic when oLPN is packed ( oLPN Packed ) . This is for conveyable divisions only . If there is shortage, Dematic may request replenishment for the location. WMOS processes the message and reprioritizes existing replenishments. If none exists, WMOS creates replenishment. If Dematic requirement cannot be fulfilled, no message is sent to Dematic . Upon fill pick in requested location, WMOS sends replenishment complete. Once packing complete, the oLPN is inducted which may be processed for audit by Dematic . During Dematic audit , Dematic sends message if expected inventory cannot be located. Processing this custom message in WMOS is accomplished through EX02 MHE oLPN Adjustments . WMOS performs down adjustments for the oLPN line. If the oLPN content needs to be moved to another oLPN, fully or partially, Dematic sends a message to WMOS for each line moved to each destination oLPN. Processing this custom message in WMOS is accomplished through EX03 MHE Move LPN Content . WMOS performs oLPN line move to the destination oLPN. Messages Line I tem Status oLPN Packed oLPN Down Adjustment oLPN Split-Move oLPN Cancelled Replenishment Request Replenishment Complete WMOS RF Picking Picking in WMOS is executed using RF device and label picking. As an alternative to label picking, McLane picks from active using RF device . Once the conveyable oLPN is packed, WMOS sends oLPN Packed message ( oLPN Packed ) . In case of shortage, oLPN Packed message . Messages oLPN Packed Shipping – Non- Conveyable Divisions In non-conveyable divisions, Dematic palletizes the PTL oLPNs . This information is sent to WMOS. Through EX22 MHE Palletize oLPN , WMOS palletizes the oLPN. Then the pallet is moved to dock door area for loading. T he oLPNs are loaded using WMOS RF device . Once all the oLPNs are loaded, outbound office user closes shipment. EX04 Shipment Close MHE Message sends the custom message from WMOS to Dematic . Messages MHE Palletize oLPN Shipment Closed Shipping –Conveyable Divisions In conveyable divisions, when a conveyable oLPN, either WMOS picked or PTL picked, is inducted, Dematic diverts the oLPN to corresponding dock door. PTL oLPNs are first collected in sorting lane and then released by sort wave. Dematic sends Divert scan in sorting lane to WMOS to track the last known location. Similarly, all conveyable oLPNs including the oLPNs picked using WMOS, are merged in merge lane. .

[Document: MCLN MHE Communication Document v2.8]

the oLPN to corresponding dock door. PTL oLPNs are first collected in sorting lane and then released by sort wave. Dematic sends Divert scan in sorting lane to WMOS to track the last known location. Similarly, all conveyable oLPNs including the oLPNs picked using WMOS, are merged in merge lane. .

Dematic sends divert scan in merge lane to WMOS to track the last known location. Finally, when the oLPN passes down lane, Dematic send another divert message. Using this message, WMOS updates the oLPN to “Loaded on Truck” status. Messages oLPN Divert Shipment Closed Overview McLane non-conveyable divisions and conveyable divisions are setup with PTL. Conveyable divisions are setup with Dematic MHE automation for outbound pre- sort lanes for PTL oLPNs , sort wave level merge lanes and shipping sorter lanes . WM OS sends wave download messages to Dematic for the Orders to be processed in Dematic . For non- conveyable divisions , oLPNs to be picked for PTL are sent ( Wave Download – Non-Conveyable ) . For conveyable divisions , all oLPNs are sent to Dematic for additional work planning in Dematic . PTL oLPNs are flagged so that Dematic can pick those oLPNs ( Wave Download –Conveyable ) . Dematic plans work in conveyable divisions and sends message to WMOS with all oLPNs and associated sort wave number ( oLPNs with Sort Wave ). Using GAP19, WMOS updates oLPNs with sort wave number and creates picking tasks. Inventory for PTL oLPNs is picked in Dematic . Dematic sends update per oLPN line when picking is complete or when picking is paused ( Line Item Status ) . The quantity on this message is an incremental quantity for the quantity packed but not the total quantity packed for the oLPN line. During Dematic QC, Dematic sends message if expected inventory cannot be located ( oLPN Down Adjustment ) . For shorting the oLPN line completely, Dematic sends the message with quantity as zero. Processing this custom message in WMOS is accomplished through EX02 MHE oLPN Adjustment . WMOS performs down adjustments for the oLPN line. If the oLPN content needs to be moved to another oLPN, fully or partially, Dematic sends a message to WMOS for each line moved to each destination oLPN ( oLPN Split-Move ) . Processing this custom message in WMOS is accomplished through EX03 MHE Move oLPN Content . WMOS performs oLPN line move to the destination oLPN. oLPN Packed Once the conveyable oLPN is packed, WMOS sends oLPN Packed message ( oLPN Packed ) . All full case conveyable oLPNs, label picked/RF picked oLPNs from active and PTL packed oLPNs are considered for this message. MHE Palletize oLPN Dematic sends the message to WMOS when the PTL oLPN is located in pallet ( MHE Palletize oLPN ) .

[Document: MCLN MHE Communication Document v2.8]

cked, WMOS sends oLPN Packed message ( oLPN Packed ) . All full case conveyable oLPNs, label picked/RF picked oLPNs from active and PTL packed oLPNs are considered for this message. MHE Palletize oLPN Dematic sends the message to WMOS when the PTL oLPN is located in pallet ( MHE Palletize oLPN ) .

Through EX22 MHE Palletize oLPN , WMOS processes this custom message to palletize the PTL oLPNs . oLPN Divert To track the last known location of the oLPN in conveyer , Dematic sends divert message to WM for tracking. The physical divert is configured as the reserve location WMOS. After processing the message, WMOS locates the oLPN in the physical divert. Similarly, the last divert for oLPN acts as loading divert; WM updates oLPN to “ Loaded on Truck ” upon receiving this divert. Replenishment If additional inventory needed to fulfil PTL oLPNs , Dematic request WM OS ( Replenishment Request ). WM processes the request and sends allocation complete message to Dematic ( Replenishment Complete ) when the fill active is executed on the location . If request cannot be fulfilled, no failure message is sent to Dematic . If oLPN is cancelled in WMOS, base message is sent to Dematic ( oLPN Cancelled ). In the case of unprocessed or failed messages, these values can be used to determine what went wrong with a specific message and where in the process the issue occurred. The EVENT_MESSAGE table displays the following statuses throughout message processing: Status Description 5 Receive message unprocessed 10 Send message unprocessed 15 Dematic receive message unprocessed 20 Dematic send message unprocessed 21 Dematic send message unprocessed wave 22 Dematic send message unprocessed shipment 40 Message in progress 90 Message sent 91 Message processed 95 Receive message error 96 Send message error The CL_ENDPOINT_QUEUE table displays the following statuses throughout message processing: Status Description 1 Not ready 2 Ready 3 Submitted 4 Resubmitted 5 Succeeded 6 Failed 10 Busy The CL_MESSAGE table does not have an associated status but contains the message contents and other helpful information. ACK Message Each message sent from WMOS to Dematic or Dematic to WMOS acknowledged back to the sender once the message successfully received. This is accomplished by returning message number back to the sender as shown in the following table.  If the sender does not receive an “ACK” they will retry the message.  If the retry also not received, the ACK message fails.  WM requires a fixed “ACK” format. Messages from WMOS to Dematic : DIF (Device Integration Framework) endpoint will be configured in the connect mode. DIF will connect to the Dematic port to create the socket connection and messages will be send over to Dematic on this socket connection.

[Document: MCLN MHE Communication Document v2.8]

ge fails.  WM requires a fixed “ACK” format. Messages from WMOS to Dematic : DIF (Device Integration Framework) endpoint will be configured in the connect mode. DIF will connect to the Dematic port to create the socket connection and messages will be send over to Dematic on this socket connection.

If DIF do not receive “ACK” on sent message within prescribed time (usually configured as 5000ms), then a resend process initiated. Resends will continue until Max Response Retry Limit (usually configured as 5) is reached. Each resend will be send after 5000ms. After Max number of retries, message will be failed in DIF. Messages from Dematic to WMOS: DIF endpoint configured in listen mode. Dematic will connect to the DIF port to create the socket connection and messages sent over to DIF on this socket connection. DIF will send back the ACK on successful receipt of the message. Heartbeat Message Heartbeat is a special message that DIF requires to keep a connection open. DIF sends/receives heartbeat message every 10 seconds (this is a configurable value).  If DIF endpoint configured in connect mode, we can configure DIF to send Heartbeat messages to Dematic at regular intervals. If DIF endpoint configured in listen mode, then Dematic can send Heartbeat messages to DIF at regular intervals. If DIF does not see a Heartbeat, the connection dropped and re-connected.  Configure WM for sending an acknowledgement for a Heartbeat message. Heartbeat is a static message.
